title: ChatStreamDataType::Unknown misleading doc — implies safe to send
description: ChatStreamDataType::Unknown doc says 'Unrecognized value — silently ignored per spec.' The phrase 'silently ignored per spec' describes server behavior when an unrecognized string appears in the data_types array of a ChatStreamEnable the client sends. However, because Unknown is a variant a client can construct (e.g., from_str returning Unknown, or matching and re-using it), a contributor may read this comment and conclude it is safe to put Unknown into data_types when constructing a ChatStreamEnable message. The actual behavior is that Unknown serializes as the literal string "unknown", which is unlikely to match any server-side capability string and will be silently dropped — but the contributor has no warning that constructing or serializing Unknown is not meaningful. Fix: reword to 'Catch-all for unrecognized wire values received from the server. Do not construct this variant; it serializes as the literal string "unknown" which is not a valid data type.' (or equivalent).
close_reason: Valid polish; deferred. Tracked in follow-up bead jmap-chat-tui-yw6v or standalone.
